description_long: This is a four-story brick hostel building with a steep, gabled roof clad in greenish-gray shingles and two prominent white chimneys. The front facade features a symmetrical arrangement of windows, ornate white trim, and decorative architectural elements, including a central gable with a pointed arch and a small circular window. The ground floor has three large arched doorways, two of which are wooden double doors with glass panes, and the third is a smaller arched entryway. Above the entrances, there are two horizontal signs with German text, suggesting commercial or hospitality use. The side and rear facades are plain, lacking windows or decorative features, and the building's massing is rectangular with a slight protrusion at the rear. The overall appearance is reminiscent of late 19th or early 20th-century European urban architecture, blending functional design with modest ornamentation.
description: A four-story brick hostel with a steep gabled roof, ornate front facade, arched entrances, and symmetrical windows, featuring German signage above the main doors.
description_view_0: The front view shows the ornate brick facade, symmetrical windows, three arched entrances, and two German signs above the doors.
description_view_1: The left side view reveals a plain, windowless wall with the greenish-gray roof visible above.
description_view_2: The rear view displays a flat, featureless wall with a central vertical protrusion and no windows or doors.
description_view_3: The right side view is similar to the left, showing a plain, windowless wall and the roof's slope.
attribute_signage: Two horizontal signs above the main entrances on the front facade, with white text on a gray background. The left sign reads 'FÜR ALTEN KRONER', and the right sign reads 'VANINER AKTIEN-BIER'. Both are rectangular, spanning the width of the doorways, and use a simple, sans-serif font.
attribute_entrance_design: The main entrance consists of three arched doorways on the ground floor of the front facade. Two are large, wooden double doors with glass panes, framed in white, and the third is a smaller arched entryway. The entrances are set within the brick facade and are topped by the signage. No steps, ramps, or canopies are visible.
attribute_window_arrangement: Windows are symmetrically arranged on the front facade, with three rows of rectangular windows above the ground floor and a small circular window in the central gable. Each window has white trim, and some feature decorative lintels. The side and rear facades lack windows.
attribute_architectural_style: Historicism with late 19th-century European urban influences, featuring brickwork, gabled roof, and modest decorative trim.
